title: Connect: detect a created-but-not-running Pod instead of crawling the fake bar to 99%
description: Found 2026-06-15. User pressed Connect (RTX 2000 Ada, Pod sc2vdkrk6cvb3s); the progress bar crept to 99% as if downloading, then parked. RunPod console showed the Pod 'Start for $0.24/hr' / Compute 'Not running' — RunPod accepted createPod (201) but the container never started on a host (no host slot / immediate exit). The app only polls /remote/comfy/status (wrapper health) and paints an elapsed->% time-estimate (_pct in MpiSettings.js:531 + _connectPct in shell.js, clamp 0-99) with NO RunPod runtime-status signal behind it, so 'booting slowly' and 'Pod stopped, never started' look identical: both crawl to 99%. The MPI-86 boot watchdog (5 min) did fire (yellow 'stuck on a bad host' hint + Cancel showed) so the user wasn't trapped — but the misleading 99% bar implies a download that isn't happening. Fix: while polling, also read RunPod Pod runtime status (getPod) and if the Pod is EXITED/TERMINATED/not-running after create, stop the fake progress and surface 'Pod failed to start on host — Cancel and pick another GPU' immediately rather than crawling 4 min to 99%. Distinguishes dead-host from healthy slow-boot. App-side; needs a backend getPod-status route the renderer can poll alongside /remote/comfy/status. See brief.md.
